pattern: Chunking the string into fixed-width blocks and folding each block's letters into a single alphabet position via a running sum modulo 26 builds the hashed result one character at a time.
hints: Break the string into consecutive chunks of exactly k characters; the number of chunks is fixed by the string length divided by k.
For each chunk, add up every character's alphabet position (a=0 through z=25).
Take that sum modulo 26 and convert it back to a lowercase letter, appending it to the result for that chunk.

edge_inputs.description: the entire string is a single chunk
chunk size of one hashes every character independently
a chunk sums to exactly 26, wrapping to 'a'
a chunk sums to a multiple of 26 greater than 26
repeated identical chunks should hash identically

stress.description: random lowercase strings with a chunk size that evenly divides the length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randint(1, 30)
    chunks = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(30, (size // k) + 1)))
    n = k * chunks
    s = ''.join(chr(97 + rng.randint(0, 25)) for _ in range(n))
    return [s, k]
stress.note: length is always an exact multiple of k, as guaranteed by the problem
